title: GraspSearchRequest
description: BFF -> grasp-search service input. Wire form uses camelCase.

contractVersion.description: Internal contract version (see contract-version.json). Mismatch is rejected with 400.

layoutVersion.description: Layout DSL schema version this declaration targets, e.g. 'layout/1.0'. References the public schema; not redefined here.

graspSearchDeclaration.description: The graspSearch declaration. Its detailed shape (hardConstraints / objectives) is owned by the Layout DSL schema named by layoutVersion; kept open here on purpose.

objectiveWeights.description: objective name -> weight. Keys correspond to score breakdown keys.

topN.description: How many ranked candidates to return (fallback material for the BFF).

robot.description: Declares where the robot base is (ADR-083). The engine already used this as the reach/IK reference point via the open payload; this makes it a typed, optional field. Reach/IK evaluation itself stays solved in core/ -- this only declares the base pose.

base.description: [x, y, z] world-frame position of the robot base (ROS convention, Z up).

camera.description: Declares the vision camera for the 'can it be seen' domain gate (ADR-081). Like `robot`, this only declares -- visibility evaluation itself is solved in core/. Optional: with no camera declared the visibility stage passes everything (rejectedByVisibility stays 0 and `visible` is vacuously true).

position.description: [x, y, z] world-frame position of the camera's optical center.

viewAxis.description: Optional viewing direction (need not be unit length; the solver normalizes). The field-of-view cone applies only when both viewAxis and fovHalfAngle are declared.

fovHalfAngle.description: Optional field-of-view half angle in radians (cone around viewAxis).

gripper.description: Declares the parallel-jaw gripper for the 'can it be grasped' domain gate (ADR-081). Declaration only -- the geometric grasp gate itself is solved in core/. Optional: with no gripper declared the grasp stage passes everything (rejectedByGrasp stays 0 and `graspable` is vacuously true).

maxOpening.description: Maximum jaw opening width (same length unit as the request geometry).

fingerClearance.description: Extra clearance the fingers need to slide in beside the target (the naive gate requires maxOpening >= target width + fingerClearance).
